title: Estate Settlement Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Estimate Estate Settlement Costs & Attorney Fees
description: Calculate total estate settlement costs including attorney fees, taxes & court fees. Free tool updated for 2026.
intro: Estate settlement can be complex and costly. This calculator estimates the total cost of settling an estate with real property, including attorney fees, taxes, and administrative expenses. Knowing these costs upfront helps you plan and avoid unexpected bills. Figures are updated for 2026 and reviewed periodically.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **6–10% of estate value**. Direct calculation with your input values. Total cost = attorney fees + taxes + administrative expenses.
useCases: Technical calculations for estate settlement costs and attorney fees
Students and professionals in law and estate planning
Verify estimated costs before speaking with an attorney
Teaching and learning estate settlement concepts
explanation:
Estate settlement involves official procedures with costs that vary by jurisdiction, processing times, and required documentation. Understanding these costs upfront saves time and prevents unexpected expenses.

## How It's Calculated

Total estate settlement cost = attorney fees + taxes + administrative expenses.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking against typical ranges and alerting you if values seem unusual.

## Important Notes

This calculation is an estimate for reference only. For major financial, legal, or tax decisions, consult with a qualified attorney or tax professional. Values are current for 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: Who pays for estate settlement costs?
faq.a: Costs are deducted from the estate before distributing assets to heirs and beneficiaries.

faq.q: How much do attorney fees cost for estate settlement?
faq.a: Attorney fees typically range from 8–15% of the estate value, depending on case complexity and state requirements.

faq.q: What estate and inheritance taxes apply?
faq.a: Tax rates vary significantly by state. Some states have no inheritance tax; others use progressive rates based on heir relationship and the amount received.

faq.q: Are there court or filing fees?
faq.a: Yes. Most jurisdictions charge 1–2% of the estate value for court costs and filing fees.

faq.q: What happens if there's no will?
faq.a: Without a will, state law determines the order of heirs (children, spouse, parents). The process is typically longer and costs more.

faq.q: How long does estate settlement take?
faq.a: Timeline varies: simple estates typically take 6–12 months; complex estates may take 2–3 years or longer. Expedited options may be available with additional costs.

faq.q: Can I handle estate settlement online?
faq.a: Some steps (state filings, tax returns) can be done online, but most require in-person appearances and professional legal representation.

faq.q: What's the difference between probate and estate settlement?
faq.a: Probate is the legal court process. Estate settlement includes probate, taxes, asset distribution, and closing all accounts.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data update required
